publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-22 00:13:01 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Black Panther collection

origination.label: Creator
origination.persname: Davis, Angela Y. (Angela Yvonne), 1944- (Title of work: If they come in the morning..)

did.abstract: Publications, posters, handbills, flyers and news clippings covering the activities of the Black Panthers and several of its members. Publications include issues of the periodicals The Black Panther, Black Panther community newsletter, Liberated guardian, Open city, and The Berkeley tribe, and pamphlets by Huey P. Newton and Kim Il Sung. Many of the posters, handbills, and flyers concern Huey P. Newton, Bobby Seale, or Eldridge Cleaver. Other items include a cover proof for If they come in the morning by Angela Y. Davis.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Publications, posters, handbills, flyers and news clippings covering the activities of the Black Panthers and several of its members. Publications include issues of the periodicals The Black Panther, Black Panther community newsletter, Liberated guardian, Open city, and The Berkeley tribe, and pamphlets by Huey P. Newton and Kim Il Sung. Many of the posters, handbills, and flyers concern Huey P. Newton, Bobby Seale, or Eldridge Cleaver. Other items include a cover proof for If they come in the morning by Angela Y. Davis.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Black Panther Collection, #8069. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Publications
Subseries Ia. Black Panther Community News Service
No. 4, You Can't Jail a Revolution
Vol. 1, No. 6, Huey Must be Set Free!
Vol. 3, No. 21, Next Victim of Fascist Frameup?
Vol. 4, No. 3, By lifting their hands against these revolutionaries
Vol. 2, No. 2, Free All Black Political Prisoners
Vol. 2, No. 23, Happy Birthday Huey
Vol. 2, No. 25, Viva Zapata 1883-1919
Vol. 2, No. 28, Chairman Bobby Seale Returns From Scandinavia
Vol. 3, No. 8, Capitalism Plus Racism Breeds Fascism
Vol. 3, No. 9, Reagan's Fascist Pigs Vamp on Sacramento HQ
Vol. 3, No. 11, Eldridge Cleaver Breaks His Silence
Vol. 3, No. 12
Vol. 3, No. 14, United Against Fascism
Vol. 3, No. 22, Our Fight Is Not in Vietnam
Vol 3, No. 24, Bobby Seale, Chairman
Vol. 3, No. 28, Political Prisoners of USA Fascism
Vol. 4, No. 4, Although my body may be bound and shackled…
Vol. 4, No. 8, The Invincible Thoughts of The Black Panther Party Can Never Be Destroyed By Fire Nor Water,
Vol. 4, No. 10, Happy Birthday Huey
Vol. 4, No. 14, Hunter's Point 1967; Hunter's Point 1970
Vol. 4, No. 18, He Was The Beginning: Lil Bobby Hutton
Vol. 4, No. 20, "This Is But An Aspect Of The Unfolding Picture Of American Fascism,"
Vol. 4, No. 22, 'The Black Panther Party is the People's Party and We Are Primarily Interested in Freeing Man.'
Vol. 4, No. 24, The Heirs of Malcolm Have Picked Up The Gun and Now Stand Millions Strong Facing The Racist Pig Oppressor
Vol. 4, No. 25 and 26, Babylon,
Vol. 5, No. 7, Revolutionary Suicide
Vol. 5, No. 8, If The Penalty for the Quest for Freedom is Death—Then by Death We Escape to Freedom
Vol. 5, No. 10, Lonnie McLucas
Vol. 5, No. 17, Rent-A-Pid Kills 14 Year Old Brother In Homemade Playground
Vol. 5, No. 20, On The Battlefield In Cairo, Illinois
Vol. 5, No. 24, New York City…Criminal Slumlords Are Crime Partners With City Government Officials and Courts
Vol. 5, No. 26, The Last Christmas???
Vol. 5, No. 28, In the spirit of revolutionary solidarity
Subseries Ia. Black Panther Intercommunal News Service
Vol. 6, No. 5, The San Quentin Branch of The Black Panther Party Opens!
Vol. 6, No. 9, The World is Yours, As Well As Ours
Vol. 6, No. 11, Black Genocide: Sickle Cell Anemia
Vol. 7. No. 12, World War III?
Vol. 7, No. 19, Cease-fire Verdict in L.A.
Vol. 7, No. 25, Another Mother For Struggle
Vol. 7, No. 28. Welcome Home, Angela Davis
Vol. 7, No. 29, The Glass Cage Must Come Down!
Vol. 8, No. 5, No Eviction, Free Repairs—If You Were Your Own Landlord
Vol. 8, No. 21, Survival Pending Liberation
Vol. 9, No. 26, Vote April 17th
Center spread only, "24 Hour Vigils for Eldridge Cleaver"

Right On! Revolutionary Peoples Communication Network. 32 Political Prisoners Murdered in Attica Prison

Hustler!

Vrÿheidsschool, Rozenstraat 6: Alle macht aan het volk

Notes on the Black Panther Party for Self Defense and the Peace and Freedom Movement
Notes on the Black Panther Party for Self Defense and the Peace and Freedom Movement

origination.corpname: United Defense to Free Political Prisoners

Where's the Conspiracy, Free the Panther 21
It's a Free Country… Isn't it?
Black People Boycott
People's No. 1 Enemy
Free the Panther 21, Free All Political Prisoners

did.unittitle: Relocations
First You… Now Your Children…
Free the N.Y. 21
Over the Bridge; On to the Jail; People's Power; Is the Panther's Bail!

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: advertising march in Queens, New York to free the Panther 21, Bobby Seale, and all political prisoners

did.unittitle: Humanity, Freedom, Peace

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: New World Liberation Front|Afro-American Liberation Army

did.unittitle: Black News, Vol. 2, No. 1
Angela Davis Newsletter: Harris Still Fails to Implicate Angela

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Philadelphia Committee To Free Angela Davis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains article regarding Angela Davis' charges and trial in regards to the Soledad Brothers, as well as a list of happenings (rallies, fundraisers, and other events), and bookstores related to the Black Panther Party in Philadelphia.

did.unittitle: John Huggins Free Hot Breakfast Program for Children
Sickle Cell Anemia
The Black Panther Party, William Kunstler, and Dave Dellinger are Not Speaking at Bryant Park Today Because…
The Black Panther Intercommunal New Service

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: This special issue of the Black Panther Party newspaper was printed in Vietnamese and features an illustration of a member of the National Liberation Front (Viet Cong)

did.unittitle: Free David Hilliard, Free All Political Prisoners

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text on poster reads, "Railroaded to prison: June 12, 1971. There were no Black People on the jury in the case of David Hilliard, although 38% of the Oakland Community is Black."

did.unittitle: "The Black Panther Party 40th Year Reunion and Celebration"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains full color reproduction of illustration by Emory Douglas, Ministry of Culture for the Black Panther Party, and information regarding the Black Panther Party reunion in Oakland, California. Two copies.

did.unittitle: Here and Now For Bobby Seale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text reads, "Here and Now For Bobby Seale, We must save Bobby Seale because we must save the Black Panther Party because we must save the revolutionary spirit in America."

did.unittitle: On the 13th Anniversary of the Assassination of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., Join Us In A March Against Racist Violence!
Ramparts
"War Sucks" button
"All Power to The People" and photograph of Huey P. Newton button
Button with sepia print photograph of Bobby Hutton, first member of the Black Panther Party
Button with black peace sign on white background.
Button with sepia print photograph of Eldridge Cleaver
